m_Item1.QuestionText: What is overfitting in machine learning?

Options.OptionText: When the model performs well on unseen data
When the model generalizes well to new data
When the model learns noise or irrelevant patterns from the training data
When the model is too simple to capture the underlying patterns in the data

m_Item1.Explanation: When model learns noise or irrelevant pattern from training data and fails to perform well on unseen data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Identify the kind of learning algorithm for  “facial identities for facial expressions”.
m_Item1.Explanation: Pattern Recognition is the kind of learning algorithm for  “facial identities for facial expressions”.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is machine learning?

Options.OptionText: Programming computers to perform specific tasks
Training computers to learn from data without explicit instructions
Building complex hardware systems for data processing
Creating new programming languages for scientific computing

m_Item1.Explanation: Machine learning is a subset of AI in which machine learns from the data without being explicitly programmed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT a common type of machine learning problem?
m_Item1.Explanation: Solving mathematical equations is NOT a common type of machine learning problem.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT a common metric for evaluating machine learning models?
m_Item1.Explanation: User satisfaction is NOT a common metric for evaluating machine learning models.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The Father of Machine Learning is
m_Item1.Explanation: The Father of Machine Learning is Geoffery Everent Hinton.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following are common classes of problems in machine learning?
m_Item1.Explanation: Regression, Classification, Clustering are common classes of problems in machine learning.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Among the following options identify the one which is false regarding regression.
m_Item1.Explanation: Regression problems cannot discover casual relationship.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Identify the successful applications of ML.

Options.OptionText: Learning to classify new austronomical structure
Learning to drive an autonomous vehicle

m_Item1.QuestionText: Analysis of ML algorithm needs
m_Item1.Explanation: Analysis of ML algorithm needs both statistical learning theory and computational learning theory.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The full form of PCA is
m_Item1.Explanation: The full form of PCA is Principle Component Analysis.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Unsupervised learning focuses on:

Options.OptionText: Classifying data points into predefined categories
Identifying hidden patterns and structures in data

m_Item1.Explanation: In unsupervised learning, hidden patterns are discovered from unlabbeled data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a ML model?
m_Item1.Explanation: SVG is not a ML model.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is Machine learning?

Options.OptionText: The autonomous acquisition of knowledge through the use of computer programs
The autonomous acquisition of knowledge through the use of manual programs
The selective acquisition of knowledge through the use of computer programs
The selective acquisition of knowledge through the use of manual programs

m_Item1.Explanation: Machine learning is a autonomous acquisition of knowledge through the use of computer programs.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Application of Machine learning is __________.
m_Item1.Explanation: All of the above are the applications of ML.

m_Item1.QuestionText: _________ is the machine learning algorithms that can be used with labeled data.
m_Item1.Explanation: Regression algorithms is the machine learning algorithms that can be used with labeled data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: _______ is the scenario when the model fails to decipher the underlying trend in the input data.
m_Item1.Explanation: Underfitting is the scenario when the model fails to decipher the underlying trend in the input data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The supervised learning problems can be grouped as_______.
m_Item1.Explanation: The supervised learning problems can be grouped as classification and regression based on type of target variable.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The term machine learning was coined by __________.
m_Item1.Explanation: The term machine learning was coined by Arthur Samuel.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which machine learning models are trained to make aseries of decisions based on the rewards and feedback they receive for their actions?
m_Item1.Explanation: Reinforcement learning models are trained to make aseries of decisions based on the rewards and feedback they receive for their actions.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Real-Time decisions, Game AI, Learning Tasks, Skill acquisition, and Robot Navigation are applications of .............
m_Item1.Explanation: Real-Time decisions, Game AI, Learning Tasks, Skill acquisition, and Robot Navigation are applications of Reinforcement Learning.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The Bayes rule can be used in ................
m_Item1.Explanation: The Bayes rule can be used in answering probabilistic query

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is called the average squared difference between classifier predicted output and actual output?
m_Item1.Explanation: The average squared difference between classifier predicted output and actual output is called Root mean squared error.

m_Item1.QuestionText: ................... algorithms enable the computers to learn from data, and even improve themselves, without being explicitly programmed.
m_Item1.Explanation: Machine Learning algorithms enable the computers to learn from data, and even improve themselves, without being explicitly programmed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a supervised learning?
m_Item1.Explanation: PCA is not a supervised learning.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the disadvantage of decision trees?

Options.OptionText: Decision trees are robust to outliers
Decision trees are prone to be overfit

m_Item1.Explanation: Decision trees are prone to be overfit is the disadvantage of decision tree.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If machine learning model output involves target variable then that model is called as
m_Item1.Explanation: If machine learning model output involves target variable then that model is called as Predictive model.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What characterize unlabeled examples in machine learning

Options.OptionText: there is plenty of confusing knowledge

m_Item1.Explanation: In case of unsupervised learning, we don't have labeled data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Data used to build a data mining model.
m_Item1.Explanation: Training data is used to build a data mining model.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The problem of finding hidden structure in unlabeled data is called…
m_Item1.Explanation: The problem of finding hidden structure in unlabeled data is called unsupervised learning.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Of the Following Examples, Which would you address using an supervised learning Algorithm?

Options.OptionText: given email labeled as spam or not spam, learn a spam filter
given a set of news articles found on the web, group them into set of articles about the same story.
given a database of customer data, automatically discover market segments and group customers into different market segments.
find the patterns in market basket analysis

m_Item1.Explanation: Email spam classification is a supervised machine learning example.

m_Item1.QuestionText: You are given reviews of few netflix series marked as positive, negative and neutral. Classifying reviews of a new netflix series is an example of
m_Item1.Explanation: Review classification is a supervised learning.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a good test dataset characteristic?

Options.OptionText: large enough to yield meaningful results
is representative of the dataset as a whole

m_Item1.Explanation: A good test dataset should be large enough to yield meaningful results and it should be representative of the dataset as a whole.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A feature F1 can take certain value: A, B, C, D, E, & F and represents grade of students from a college. Here feature type is
m_Item1.Explanation: The grades is a Ordinal Categorical data type.

m_Item1.QuestionText: PCA is
m_Item1.Explanation: The PCA is used for Feature Extraction.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following techniques would perform better for reducing dimensions of a data set?

Options.OptionText: removing columns which have too many missing values
removing columns which have high variance in data
removing columns with dissimilar data trends

m_Item1.Explanation: Removing columns which have too many missing values will be correct option for reducing dimensions of data set.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a type of neural network?
m_Item1.Explanation: Convolutional neural network (CNN) is type of Neural Network.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is an example of a classification problem?

Options.OptionText: Predicting the price of a house based on its features
Predicting the weight of a person based on their height
Predicting whether a customer will churn or not
Predicting the age of a person based on their income

m_Item1.Explanation: Customer churn prediction comes under classification problem.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is an example of a clustering algorithm?
m_Item1.Explanation: K-means is type of clustering algorithm.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is an example of a dimensionality reduction technique?
m_Item1.Explanation: Principal component analysis (PCA) is an example of a dimensionality reduction technique.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the confusion matrix in machine learning?

Options.OptionText: To visualize the distribution of the data in a dataset
To compare the performance of different models
To evaluate the performance of a classification model
To evaluate the performance of a regression model

m_Item1.Explanation: Confusion matrix is a performance metrics for classification model.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is an example of a supervised learning problem?
m_Item1.Explanation: Image classification is an example of a supervised learning problem.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is an example of a deep learning architecture?
m_Item1.Explanation: Convolutional neural network (CNN) is an example of a deep learning architecture.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a common activation function used in deep learning?
m_Item1.Explanation: Sigmoid is a common activation function used in deep learning.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a common approach to handling missing data in machine learning?

Options.OptionText: Filling in the missing data with a constant value
Filling in the missing data with the mean or median value
Using a separate imputation model to predict the missing values

m_Item1.Explanation: Imputation method is mainly used to fill null values.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a common approach to reducing dimensionality in machine learning?
m_Item1.Explanation: Feature extraction is a common approach to reducing dimensionality in machine learning.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the process of feeding data to a machine learning model to improve its performance called?
m_Item1.Explanation: Training is the process of feeding data to a machine learning model to help it learn and improve its performance.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the context of machine learning, what is the difference between a feature and a label?

Options.OptionText: A feature is the input data, and a label is the desired output.
A feature is the output data, and a label is the model used.
There is no difference; they are the same thing.
A feature is the color of a button, and a label is the text on the button.

m_Item1.Explanation: A feature is the input data, and a label is the desired output.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is an example of a regression problem?

Options.OptionText: Predicting whether an email is spam or not
Forecasting the future sales of a product.
Identifying handwritten digits in an image
Grouping customers based on their purchase history

m_Item1.Explanation: As Sales is a numeric value (continous value) forecasting the future sales of a product is a regression problem.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the term for a machine learning model that can improve its performance over time without explicit programming?
m_Item1.Explanation: Self-learning machine learning is artificial intelligence that can train itself using unlabeled data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary difference between a linear regression model and a logistic regression model?

Options.OptionText: Linear regression predicts continuous values, while logistic regression predicts probabilities.
Linear regression is for supervised learning, while logistic regression is for unsupervised learning.
Logistic regression uses more complex calculations compared to linear regression.
Linear regression can only handle numerical data, while logistic regression can handle categorical data as well.

m_Item1.Explanation:
Linear regression is a supervised learning algorithm used for continuous prediction tasks. It finds a best-fitting line or hyperplane to model the relationship between independent variables (features) and a dependent variable (target).

Logistic regression, on the other hand, is used for classification tasks. It predicts the probability of an event occurring, typically falling between 0 (not likely) and 1 (very likely).

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the fundamental unit of a neural network?
m_Item1.Explanation: Perceptron is a fundamental unit of a neural network.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the main difference between a feedforward neural network and a recurrent neural network (RNN)?

Options.OptionText: Feedforward networks have only one hidden layer, while RNNs can have multiple.
Feedforward networks process information only in one direction, while RNNs can process information sequentially.
Feedforward networks are better suited for image recognition, while RNNs are better for language processing.
Feedforward networks require more training data compared to RNNs.

m_Item1.Explanation: Feedforward networks process information only in one direction, while RNNs can process information sequentially. (RNNs can handle temporal dependencies in data)

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a technique used for identifying patterns in data by training a model on a dataset and using it to make predictions on new data?
m_Item1.Explanation: Machine Learning is a technique used for identifying patterns in data by training a model on a dataset and using it to make predictions on new data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a type of machine learning  algorithm in which the model learns through trial and error by receiving feedback on its performance?
m_Item1.Explanation: Reinforcement Learning is a type of machine learning  algorithm in which the model learns through trial and error by receiving feedback on its performance.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a measure of how well a machine learning model is able to make predictions on new data?
m_Item1.Explanation: All above mentioned are all performance merics of ML models.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a technique for predicting a continuous target variable?
m_Item1.Explanation: Regression is a technique for predicting a continuous target variable.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a measure of how much a dependent variable changes when an independent variable changes?
m_Item1.Explanation: Slope is change in one variable to change in another variable.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a common machine learning algorithm for classification?
m_Item1.Explanation: Linear regression is a regression model.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a common machine learning algorithm for regression?
m_Item1.Explanation: Linear regression is a common machine learning algorithm.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a technique for dealing with missing data?
m_Item1.Explanation: Missing data can be filled using imputation techniques.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a technique for detecting outliers in a dataset?
m_Item1.Explanation: Box plot is used to detect the outliers in dataset.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a technique for selecting the most important features in a dataset?
m_Item1.Explanation: Feature Selection is a technique for selecting the most important features in a dataset.
